onboarding.namePrompt: Let's personalize your experience. First, what's your name?
onboarding.experienceDesc: This helps us show you the right tools and hide things that might overwhelm you.
onboarding.goalsDesc: Select all that apply. We'll prioritize relevant content for you.
onboarding.themeDesc: Choose how GuitarTeacher looks for you. You can always change this later.
onboarding.levelIntermediateDesc: I know some chords and can play songs
onboarding.levelAdvancedDesc: I can play lead guitar and know theory

profile.title: Your Profile
profile.subtitle: Track your progress, view stats, and manage your learning data.
profile.levelDesc: Changing your level affects which tools and features are visible. Beginner shows only essential tools; Advanced shows everything.
profile.levelBeginnerDesc: Essential tools for learning the basics
profile.levelIntermediateDesc: More practice tools plus music theory
profile.levelAdvancedDesc: Full feature set including advanced theory
profile.skillTreesDesc: Unlock new abilities and track your progression
profile.editGoalsDesc: Select the goals you want to focus on in your sessions.
profile.noGoalsSet: No goals set. Edit your goals to start sessions.
profile.leftHandedDesc: Flip fretboard diagrams for left-handed players
profile.autoBackupDesc: Automatically download a backup JSON every {days} days
profile.appearanceDesc: Choose your theme and accent color
profile.profilesDesc: Switch between family members or practice personas
profile.deleteProfileConfirm: Delete this profile? All data for this profile will be lost.
profile.exportDesc: Download all your progress data as a JSON file. Keep it as a backup or transfer to another device.
profile.importDesc: Restore your progress from a previously exported JSON file.
profile.importError: Invalid data format. Please check your file.
profile.resetDesc: Clear all your progress, achievements, and stats. This cannot be undone!
profile.resetConfirmDesc: This will permanently delete all your progress including:

practiceRoutine.title: Practice Routine
practiceRoutine.subtitle: A guided sequence to build your skills every day
practiceRoutine.greatPracticeDesc: You completed the full routine. Keep it up and you'll see improvement fast.
practiceRoutine.stepTuneDesc: Start every session by tuning your guitar. Take as long as you need.
practiceRoutine.stepWarmupDesc: Get your fingers moving with warm-up exercises.
practiceRoutine.stepNotesDesc: Train your fretboard knowledge. Spend 3 minutes finding notes.
practiceRoutine.stepChordsDesc: Practice switching between chords smoothly. 5 minutes of focused drilling.
practiceRoutine.stepStrummingDesc: Work on your rhythm with strumming patterns.
practiceRoutine.stepSongDesc: Apply what you practiced to a real song. Take as long as you want.
practiceRoutine.stepCooldownDesc: Nice work! Reflect on what you learned today.
practiceRoutine.stepCooldownHint: Think about which chord transitions felt smooth and which need more work.

tuner.title: Guitar Tuner
tuner.instruction1: Click "Start Tuning" and allow microphone access
tuner.instruction2: Play each string one at a time
tuner.instruction3: Watch the needle - center means in tune
tuner.instruction4: If the needle is left, tune UP (higher pitch)
tuner.instruction5: If the needle is right, tune DOWN (lower pitch)
tuner.instruction6: Click on any string to hear the reference tone

practiceTimer.title: Practice Timer
practiceTimer.pomodoroStep1: Set a timer for 25 minutes (focus time)
practiceTimer.pomodoroStep3: When timer rings, take a 5-minute break
practiceTimer.pomodoroStep4: After 4 sessions, take a longer break (15-30 min)

earTraining.title: Ear Training
earTraining.description: Listen to the chord and identify it correctly

postGame.title: Game Results
postGame.rating1: Keep At It! Practice Makes Perfect 🎯
postGame.tipTiming: Focus on timing - try tapping your foot to the beat before playing
postGame.tipWatchNotes: Watch the notes carefully - they appear before they reach the target line
postGame.tipCombo: Try to maintain your combo - consecutive hits give bonus points!
postGame.tipPerfect: Aim for the center of the target line for Perfect hits (100 points)
postGame.tipDifficulty: You're doing great! Try increasing the difficulty for more challenge
postGame.tipTuning: Make sure your guitar is in tune and the microphone can hear you clearly
postGame.tipReview: Review the lesson before playing - understanding the chord progression helps!
postGame.stringAdvice0: Try anchoring your thumb on the back of the neck for better reach on low strings
postGame.stringAdvice1: Practice transitioning to the A string with slow, deliberate finger placement
postGame.stringAdvice2: Work on D string accuracy — keep your wrist relaxed and fingers curved
postGame.stringAdvice3: G string misses are common — focus on lighter finger pressure
postGame.stringAdvice4: B string requires precision — slow down and aim deliberately
postGame.stringAdvice5: High E is thin and sensitive — use just enough pressure without pressing too hard
postGame.stringAdviceDefault: Practice this note slowly, then build up speed

session.title: Sessions
session.subtitle: Start focused practice sessions with goals
session.selectGoal: What do you want to focus on?
session.noGoalsSet: You haven't set any goals yet.

rhythm: Rhythm is the heartbeat of music. Practice with a metronome to build solid timing!
Start slow - 60 BPM - and gradually increase. Speed comes from accuracy.
Count out loud while practicing. It forces your brain and hands to sync.
Try playing with a drum backing track for real-world feel.
lead: Lead guitar is about melody and expression. Start with pentatonic scales and build from there.
Learn the minor pentatonic shape first - it works over almost everything.
Practice connecting scale positions - the whole neck becomes your canvas.
Focus on vibrato technique. It is what separates good players from great ones.
barre: Barre chords can be tricky. Remember: use your index finger as a bar, not just a clamp!
Roll your index finger slightly - don't flatten it completely.
Focus on the side of your index finger for the thickest strings.
Start with E-shape barre chords - F and B - before moving to A-shape.
fingerpicking: Fingerpicking takes patience. Start slow and focus on even tone from each finger.
Use the anchors - thumb on low strings, fingers on higher strings.
Practice the Travis picking pattern: thumb alternates, fingers add melody.
Try the "PIMA" technique: thumb (P), index (I), middle (M), ring (A).
chords: Chords are your vocabulary. Master the essential open chords first, then expand.
The "CAGED" system helps you play the same chord shape all over the neck.
Work on clean chord changes - Em to G to C is a great practice sequence.
Learn to mute strings you do not want to hear - muting is just as important as fretting.
scales: Scales build muscle memory. Practice them in different positions across the neck.
The pentatonic scale is your best friend - learn it in 5 positions.
Practice scales with a metronome, starting at 60 BPM.
Learn to play scales in thirds - it adds texture and builds coordination.
tips.title: Practice Tips
tips.subtitle: Curated advice to help you improve faster
tips.noTips: Select a goal to see relevant tips
strumming: Start with downstrokes only. Master the motion before adding complexity.
Keep your wrist relaxed and let it pivot, do not move your whole arm.
Practice the 4-beat down-up pattern until it feels natural.
Sync your strumming hand with the metronome, not the other way around.
